title: Spin-polarized Energy Density Method from Spin-Density Functional Theory
abstract: The energy density method is generalized to include spin polarization with the full formalism derived based on spin-density functional theory, which aims at decomposing the total energy into well-defined atomic energies. The method involves two steps: (1) decomposing the total energy into spin-polarized energy density functions in real space, and (2) integrating these energy densities over chosen gauge-invariant volumes for uniquely defined atomic energies, whose summation over all the atoms restores the DFT total energy up to a constant difference. This method is numerically implemented into the Vienna ab initio simulation package for the projector augmented-wave method, and is showcased with two applications. In the first application, we model the paramagnetic face-centered cubic Fe using spin special quasirandom structures; the spin energies are fit to spin cluster expansions and a deep neural network. In the second application, we calculate the atomic energy distributions of dilute magnetic semiconductor Ni-doped GaN with different dopant distances and spin configurations. This method extracts additional useful information for the study of magnetic systems with density functional theory.
summary: This paper introduces a new way to break down the total energy of a magnetic system into specific contributions from individual atoms using spin-density functional theory. By implementing this in the VASP code, the authors show it can be used to train machine learning models (like neural networks) to predict local magnetic energies. The method is demonstrated to be highly effective for studying complex magnetic environments, such as disordered iron and doped semiconductors, providing much more local detail than standard total energy calculations.
main_problem: Generalizing the Energy Density Method (EDM) to include spin polarization to decompose total energy into well-defined, spatially distributed atomic energies in magnetic systems.
main_result: The authors developed a spin-polarized EDM implemented in VASP that successfully partitions energy in magnetic systems, demonstrating high accuracy in predicting atomic energies for paramagnetic Fe using cluster expansions and deep neural networks.
method: The method decomposes total energy into spin-polarized kinetic, Coulomb, exchange-correlation, and on-site energy densities, integrated over gauge-invariant Bader and charge-neutral volumes.
model_or_system: The study applies the method to paramagnetic face-centered cubic (fcc) Fe modeled via Special Quasirandom Structures (SQS) and Ni-doped GaN dilute magnetic semiconductors.
key_observables: Spin-polarized energy densities, atomic energies, magnetization density, and magnetic moments.
important_parameters: Lattice parameter a0 = 3.573 A, plane-wave cut-off of 550 eV, and exchange parameters (J) for the Landau-SCE model.
main_assumptions: The formalism assumes collinearity of spins and neglects orbital magnetic moments and spin-orbit coupling.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows RMS error for SQS sizes; Figure 2 shows histograms of atomic magnetic moments; Figure 3 shows RMSE plots for SCE and DNN models; Figure 6 shows spatial distribution of energy/magnetization in Ni-doped GaN; Figure 7 shows energy distributions for Ni-Ni configurations.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the spin-polarized EDM formalism, details its numerical implementation in VASP, presents an application to paramagnetic Fe using machine learning (SCE and DNN), and concludes with an application to Ni-doped GaN to study localized exchange interactions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
